one_time_description: First Nation Drinking Water Settlement Revenue of $500,000 received in 2023 (non-recurring)
economic_notes: Government acquired Deer Creek Ranch in Hanceville BC for $3,500,000 through newly incorporated 1380646 BC Ltd. Yunesit'in Development Enterprises operates with a $800,000 bank overdraft facility. River West sawmill was inactive. Dandzen Development LP operates a 1 MW solar PV plant at the River West sawmill site. Cannabis retail operation opened in 2022 but not in compliance with BC Cannabis Act licensing requirements. Government operates greenhouses, a bed and breakfast, and various limited partnerships.
notes: Audit is QUALIFIED because auditors could not obtain financial information for Elhdaqox Developments Ltd. (65% owned GBE), unable to determine if adjustments required to investment balance. Consolidated subsidiaries include 1380646 BC Ltd. (acquired Deer Creek Ranch for $3.5M), Yunesit'in Development Enterprises, Yunesit'in Forest to Frame GP/LP, and Yunesit'in Leading Edge GP/LP. GBEs on modified equity include Elhdaqox Developments Ltd., Yunesit'in Master GP/LP, Yunesit'in Greenworks GP/LP, Yunesit'in B&B GP/LP. Portfolio cost investments include River West Forest Products (22%) and Dandzen Development LP (17%). Cannabis retail operation opened in 2022 is non-compliant with BC Cannabis Act - contingent liability undetermined. Fiscal year is 2023 not 2024 as stated in document header. Total government transfers estimated as ISC $7,726,700 + FNHA $1,008,706 + Province of BC $128,207 + FNESC $496,498 + Cariboo Chilcotin ATEC $232,996 + CMHC $1,456 + Tsilhqot'in National Government $732,754 = $10,327,317; OSR = total revenue minus government transfers. Net debt of $(718,165) reported on statement of changes in net debt. Tangible capital assets net book value not extractable from provided text.
